channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Three NT child protection workers stood down as inquiry into Kumanjayi Little Baby case begins
item.description: Three Northern Territory child protection workers have been stood down as the government investigates how authorities handled welfare reports concerning Kumanjayi Little Baby before the five‑year‑old ...

item.title: Pakistan Navy assists Indian vessel stranded in Arabian Sea after technical failure
item.description: In a commendable instance of cross-border maritime assistance, the Pakistan Navy responded promptly to a distress call from an Indian cargo vessel, MV Gautam, which was stranded in the Arabian Sea due...

item.title: Strictly Come Dancing confirms returning and new professional dancers for 2026 series
item.description: The BBC has revealed the professional dancer line-up for the upcoming 2026 series of Strictly Come Dancing, following the departure of five dancers earlier this year. Luba Mushtuk, Nadiya Bychkova, Ka...

item.title: CBS Series Tracker Moves Production from Vancouver to California Due to Tax Incentive
item.description: After three successful seasons filmed in Vancouver and across southern British Columbia, the CBS series Tracker will begin production of its fourth season in Los Angeles. The move comes after Disney T...

item.title: Osun State Denies Allegations of Infrastructure Fraud Against Government Officials
item.description: The Osun State Government don come out strongly to reject claims say some officials under Governor Ademola Adeleke dey involved in multi-billion naira infrastructure fraud and mismanagement of public ...

item.title: Four Dead in Clashes Between BJP and TMC Supporters After BJP Victory in West Bengal Assembly Polls
item.description: According to reports from Dawn, four persons have been killed in political violence in West Bengal following the victory of Narendra Modi's Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) in the state assembly elections...

item.title: Yanga aims to strengthen top spot while Simba SC pursues points
item.description: Today marks a crucial day for the Tanzania Mainland Premier League as Yanga and Simba SC get back into action. Yanga, top of the league with an unbeaten record of 48 points from 20 games, are set to f...

item.title: Quick-service restaurants face steeper declines in profitability amid rising cost-of-living pressures in Canada
item.description: According to a new report from Restaurants Canada, quick-service restaurants are experiencing greater difficulties than full-service and fine-dining establishments as Canadians adjust their spending d...

item.title: Michael Jackson’s chimpanzee Bubbles lives safely at the Centre of Great Apes, AI images caused confusion
item.description: Bubbles, the famous chimpanzee once owned by Michael Jackson, continues to live safely at the Centre of Great Apes in Wauchula, Florida. He has been at the centre since 2005 after becoming too big and...

item.title: AFP monitors Chinese naval and coast guard vessels in West Philippine Sea
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) reported monitoring a total of 62 Chinese naval and coast guard vessels in the West Philippine Sea throughout April 2022. These vessels were tracked across fo...

item.title: Six Cattle Killed in Unprovoked Attack on Herders in Plateau State
item.description: Yesterday for Jol community, Riyom Local Government Area, Plateau State, some herders were grazing their cows when gunmen suddenly open fire. Thankfully, no herder waka go die, but six cattle dem kill...

item.title: Prolific thief Paul Hughes, 59, admits string of thefts and fraud from London gym lockers
item.description: A 59-year-old career criminal, Paul Hughes, is facing jail once more after admitting a series of thefts and fraud offences targeting affluent individuals at luxury gyms and health clubs in the City of...

item.title: Strictly Come Dancing announces returning professionals and new line-up for 2026 series
item.description: BBC's Strictly Come Dancing has revealed the professional dancers returning for the 24th series following a significant reshuffle. Dianne Buswell returns after maternity leave, having given birth to h...

item.title: Court Suspends Jail Term for Khawula Pending Apology to Malemas
item.description: The Johannesburg High Court on Tuesday declared social media commentator Musa Khawula in contempt for failing to apologise to EFF leader Julius Malema and his wife, Mantoa Malema, for defamation. On 1...

item.title: BJP Achieves Decisive Win in West Bengal; Suvendu Adhikari Emerges as Key Leader
item.description: In a dramatic turn of events in West Bengal, the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has secured a commanding majority in the recent assembly elections, winning over 200 seats and surpassing the long-standin...

item.title: Kenya Faces Temporary Fuel Shortage Due to Technical Glitch, Government Assures Quick Resolution
item.description: The recent fuel shortages at some petrol stations in Kenya were caused by a technical and administrative glitch, according to Energy Cabinet Secretary Opiyo Wandayi. The Ministry of Energy and Petrole...

item.title: Astrologers Suggest Certain Zodiac Signs May Have a Natural Advantage in Games
item.description: Astrologer Inbaal Honigman, in collaboration with PokerTube, explored whether zodiac signs influence gaming abilities, particularly in board games, card games, and trivia like 30 Seconds. According to...

item.title: Human Rights Report Highlights Gaps in Tanzania Election Violence Investigation
item.description: The commission set up to look into the violence that happened during and after Tanzania's October 2025 general elections submitted its report to President Samia Suluhu Hassan on April 23. The chair, M...

item.title: House of Representatives secures at least 180 votes to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte
item.description: According to the House of Representatives, at least 180 lawmakers are ready to vote in favour of impeaching Vice President Sara Duterte on May 11, 2026. This number exceeds the required one-third thre...

item.title: Queensland ministers maintain privacy over integrity advice on past relationship
item.description: Two Queensland ministers, Tim Mander and Amanda Camm, have faced scrutiny over a rekindled romantic relationship. Questions arose regarding whether they had sought advice from the state's Integrity Co...
